title: Config struct + argv builder in src/config.rs; wire lib.rs
description:
- Create `src/config.rs` with a `Config` struct covering the ported flags: `system_prompt: Option<String>`, `append_system_prompt: Option<String>`, `model: Option<String>`, `allowed_tools: Vec<String>`, `disallowed_tools: Vec<String>`, `continue_session: bool`, `resume: Option<String>`, plus placeholder env/cwd override fields the isolation seam (CC.1.B) will use later (do NOT implement isolation).
- Add a public argv builder `Config::build_args(&self, prompt: &str) -> Vec<String>` that ports the flag mapping from `../../portfolio/claude-sdk-rs/src/core/config.rs` (`-p <prompt>`, `--system-prompt`, `--append-system-prompt`, `--model`, `--allowedTools`, `--disallowedTools`, `--continue`, `--resume <id>`) and ALWAYS appends `--output-format json`. Drop the security-validator / MAX_SYSTEM_PROMPT_LENGTH / telemetry bloat.
- In `src/lib.rs`, replace `pub mod config {}` with `pub mod config;` and add `pub use config::Config;`. Leave the other stub modules untouched for now.

title: Outcome + schema-locked parser in src/parse.rs; wire lib.rs
description:
- Create `src/parse.rs` defining `Outcome` (fields: `cost_usd` from `total_cost_usd`, a `Usage` sub-struct from the top-level `usage` object — `input_tokens`/`output_tokens`/`cache_creation_input_tokens`/`cache_read_input_tokens` — and `model: String`).
- Write a parser fn (e.g. `parse_result(json: &str) -> Result<Outcome>`) that reads TODAY's CLI schema: `total_cost_usd` at top level and the TOP-LEVEL `usage` object — NOT the stale `cost_usd`/`message.usage` shape.
- Model any content-block enum with a `#[serde(tag = "type")]` + an `Unknown` fallthrough variant (port the pattern from `../reference-repos/claude-agent-sdk-rust/src/types.rs` `ContentBlock`) so an unrecognized `type` doesn't fail the parse.
- In `src/lib.rs`, replace `pub mod parse {}` with `pub mod parse;` and add `pub use parse::Outcome;`.
- Serialized after task 1 (shares src/lib.rs).

title: execute() over tokio::process in src/execute.rs; wire lib.rs
description:
- Create `src/execute.rs` with `pub async fn execute(config: &Config, prompt: &str) -> Result<Outcome>`.
- Resolve the binary: `CLAUDE_BINARY` env var first, else `which::which("claude")`; return `Error::BinaryNotFound` when neither resolves.
- Build the command via `Config::build_args`; spawn with `tokio::process::Command` inheriting the env (auth is free on the subscription), `.kill_on_drop(true)`, capture stdout.
- Wrap the WHOLE call in a single `tokio::time::timeout` (no per-line 30s timeout); map elapsed to `Error::Timeout`.
- On success, feed stdout to `parse::parse_result` and return the `Outcome`.
- Add a `#[ignore]`d live smoke test (in `src/execute.rs` under `#[cfg(test)]`) that runs `execute` against a trivial prompt and asserts a populated `Outcome`; ignored so gated `cargo test` stays green.
- In `src/lib.rs`, replace `pub mod execute {}` with `pub mod execute;` and add `pub use execute::execute;`. Leave `pub mod isolation {}` as-is.
- Serialized after tasks 1 & 2 (needs Config + parser, shares src/lib.rs).

title: Exact-argv lock test in tests/argv.rs
description:
- Create `tests/argv.rs` (integration test) building a representative `Config` (system prompt, model, allowed/disallowed tools, resume id) and asserting the EXACT `Vec<String>` returned by `Config::build_args`, including flag order and the trailing `--output-format json`.
- Include a minimal-config case (only the prompt) asserting `-p <prompt>` + `--output-format json`.
- Owns a distinct file; depends only on the public arg builder from task 1.

title: Canned-JSON schema lock test in tests/parse_schema.rs
description:
- Create `tests/parse_schema.rs` (integration test) feeding canned CLI JSON (today's schema: top-level `total_cost_usd`, top-level `usage`, `model`) through the parser and asserting the expected `Outcome` (cost, each usage token field, model).
- Add a case whose content includes an UNKNOWN content-block `type` and assert it still parses (locks the forward-compat `Unknown` variant).
- Owns a distinct file; depends only on the public parser from task 2.

title: Validate
description: Run the Validation Commands listed in tasks.md and confirm all pass: `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo build --release` — all clean, no warnings. (The live `#[ignore]`d smoke test is not run by gated `cargo test`; verify it manually with `cargo test -- --ignored` on the subscription when desired.)
